title: How to Track Your Tinnitus Patterns Effectively
phrases: "The research is clear on this, and it contradicts almost everything popular culture teaches."
"Every resistance is information. The question is whether you're willing to read it."
"There is no version of growth that doesn't involve the dissolution of something you thought was permanent."
"The breath doesn't need your management. It needs your companionship."
interjections: Let that land for a second.
Think about that for a second.
researchers: Josef Rauschecker (Georgetown neuroscientist, tinnitus brain mapping)
existingBody:
The diagnosis was the easy part.

title: Building a Tinnitus Management Plan That Actually Works
phrases: "The contemplative traditions all point to the same thing: what you're looking for is what's looking."
"There's a difference between being alone and being with yourself. One is circumstance. The other is practice."
"There's a meaningful difference between self-improvement and self-understanding. One adds. The other reveals."
interjections: I get it. Really, I do.

title: The First 90 Days After Tinnitus Onset What to Do
phrases: "What we call 'stuck' is usually the body doing exactly what it was designed to do under conditions that no longer exist."
"Most of what passes for healing is just rearranging the furniture in a burning house."
"The contemplative traditions all point to the same thing: what you're looking for is what's looking."
"When you stop trying to fix the moment, something remarkable happens - the moment becomes workable."
interjections: And this is the part nobody talks about.

title: Long-Term Tinnitus Management Beyond the First Year
phrases: "The wellness industry sells solutions to problems it helps you believe you have."
"Reading about meditation is to meditation what reading the menu is to eating."
"You don't arrive at peace. You stop walking away from it."
interjections: Stick with this for a moment.
existingBody:
Nobody warns you about this part.

When the Body Speaks: Listening to the Language of Sensation

We often find ourselves caught in a relentless cycle of seeking, a desperate attempt to

title: How to Talk to Your Doctor About Tinnitus
phrases: "There is no version of growth that doesn't involve the dissolution of something you thought was permanent."
"Attention is the most undervalued resource you have. Everything else follows from where you place it."
"When you stop trying to fix the moment, something remarkable happens - the moment becomes workable."
"Embodiment is not a technique. It's what happens when you stop living exclusively in your head."
"Freedom is not the absence of constraint. It's the capacity to choose your relationship to it."
interjections: Bear with me on this one.
researchers: Bessel van der Kolk (body keeps the score, trauma and the body)
existingBody:
Three weeks in. The fridge hums. Nothing has changed except everything.
